summary: 13 film announcements, 10 TV announcements across 5 trades.

film.title: Grace
film.announcement: Millicent Simmonds co-writes and will star in the crime thriller, marking the first feature from Inevitable Studios.

film.title: Egg Baby
film.announcement: Amazon MGM Studios picked up the romantic comedy for worldwide release on Prime Video.

film.title: Mickey's Home Alone
film.announcement: Disney announced an animated 2026 Christmas special taking inspiration from the 1990s live-action Home Alone.

film.title: Thud
film.talent: Sarah Michelle Gellar (Grim Reaper); Rudy Pankow (the Devil); Mali Elfman (director)
film.announcement: Sarah Michelle Gellar and Rudy Pankow inked deals to star in the supernatural romance based on an original concept.

film.title: Monsanto Courtroom Drama
film.talent: Jonathan Bailey; Laura Dern; LaKeith Stanfield; Julia McDermott; David Duchovny; Greg Kinnear; John Lee Hancock (director)
film.announcement: John Lee Hancock's Netflix courtroom drama on the 2018 Monsanto case added six cast members to join Jonathan Bailey and Laura Dern.

film.title: Lady Macbeth
film.announcement: Curious Gremlin and Kari Skogland's Take A Trip Media are developing a feature adaptation of Ava Reid's gothic feminist retelling of Shakespeare's Macbeth.

film.title: The Midnight Library
film.announcement: Studiocanal revealed international sales for the Florence Pugh-starring drama adaptation of Matt Haig's novel, with Paramount winning U.S. bidding war.

film.title: Black Diamond
film.announcement: The folk horror comic book series is being adapted into a feature film with Ernest Dickerson directing.

film.title: Shatter Me
film.announcement: Warner Bros. acquired the rights to Tahereh Mafi's bestselling dystopian YA series for film adaptation.

film.title: Shots! Shots! Shots!
film.announcement: Amazon MGM Studios picked up the Jason Momoa action comedy, which was previously at Universal Studios.

film.title: Untitled Jan. 6 Friendship Drama
film.talent: Bradley Cooper (star, in talks); Sean Penn (director)
film.announcement: Sean Penn is set to direct Bradley Cooper in a friendship drama about a police officer, with Warner Bros. negotiating an acquisition deal.

film.title: Narnia
film.announcement: Sony Pictures will distribute Greta Gerwig and Netflix's Narnia film internationally in theaters, arriving in February.

tv.title: The Audicity
tv.announcement: Miriam Shor has joined the Season 2 cast as FBI Agent Randy Flemming, officially cast after being introduced in the Season 1 finale.

tv.title: Mickey's Home Alone
tv.announcement: Disney announced an animated 2026 Christmas special taking inspiration from the 1990s live-action Home Alone.

tv.title: Funny AF with Kevin Hart
tv.announcement: Netflix renewed the comedy competition series for a second season amid Emmy campaigns for both the series and its host.

tv.title: The Pinnacle
tv.talent: George Stephanopoulos and Ali Wentworth (producers); Damon Cardalis and Jamieson Baker (writers, executive producers)
tv.announcement: Hulu put in development The Pinnacle, a high-end New York real estate drama executive produced by George Stephanopoulos and Ali Wentworth.

tv.title: Dirty
tv.talent: Phoebe Dynevor (Detective Eva Bennett); Samantha Morton (cast); Matt Charman (writer)
tv.announcement: Samantha Morton has been cast alongside Phoebe Dynevor in Matt Charman's Amazon crime thriller series set in Manchester.

tv.title: Count My Lies
tv.talent: Lindsay Lohan (lead); François Arnaud (recurring role, Danny)
tv.announcement: François Arnaud has joined the cast of the upcoming Hulu limited series in a recurring role working alongside Lindsay Lohan.

tv.title: Heartstopper Forever
tv.announcement: Netflix dropped trailer for Heartstopper Forever, the finale feature film concluding the love story of Nick and Charlie as they face long distance, dropping July 17.

tv.title: My Adventures With Superman Spinoff
tv.announcement: Auliʻi Cravalho will star as Green Lantern in a spinoff of My Adventures with Superman, debuting in an upcoming episode.

tv.title: Ridiculous
tv.announcement: Louis C.K.'s comedy special Ridiculous will debut on Netflix on June 30, with the comic telling the story of admitting his elderly father into a retirement home.

tv.title: Hexed
tv.announcement: Disney released the first teaser for the Hailee Steinfeld-led magical adventure landing in theaters this Thanksgiving.
